title: 10 best herbal capsules for chronic hepatitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin is able to repair damaged liver cells and promote the regeneration of healthy tissue, which can help to reduce inflammation and scarring caused by chronic hepatitis. Additionally, silymarin is able to block the absorption of toxins into the bloodstream, allowing the liver to function more effectively and reducing the severity of symptoms associated with the condition.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has been shown to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and toxins, which can reduce inflammation and scarring associated with chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant activity: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to neutralize harmful oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to liver damage and disease progression in cases of chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The anti-inflammatory effects of milk thistle have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis (scarring) associated with chronic hepatitis, which can improve liver function and overall health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a complex mixture of flavonoids, particularly silibinin, that helps protect liver cells from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing liver damage and promoting regeneration in chronic hepatitis.

Silibinin: Silibinin is a major component of silymarin, exhibiting potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that inhibit the proliferation of hepatitis viruses and reduce liver fibrosis, ultimately slowing disease progression.

Isosilibinin: Isosilibinin is another bioactive flavonoid within silymarin that helps to reduce liver inflammation by inhibiting the activation of pro-inflammatory enzymes and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contributes to the resolution of liver damage.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that is believed to have hepatoprotective properties.
Leaves: They are also a source of silymarin and are sometimes used as an alternative to seeds.
Fruits: The fruits of Silybum marianum contain silymarin and are used in some herbal supplements to support liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of silybum marianum flowers and seeds from a clean dry environment for use.
Dry the silybum marianum flowers and seeds in a low-temperature oven at 50-60°c for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 10 grams of cellulose powder and 5 grams of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-300 milligrams of the uniform mixture and seal the capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The root of the dandelion plant is particularly rich in flavonoids, triterpenoid saponins, and other beneficial compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis. By supporting liver function and promoting detoxification, dandelion capsules can potentially alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression, making them a valuable complementary therapy for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Choleretic activity: Dandelion capsule's bitter compounds stimulate the liver to increase bile production, which can help alleviate symptoms of hepatitis such as jaundice and pruritus (itching).

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion capsules have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to chronic hepatitis progression.

Liver-protective effects: Dandelion's sesquiterpenes and taraxasterol have been found to exhibit hepatoprotective activity by reducing liver damage and promoting regeneration of liver cells, potentially mitigating fibrosis and cirrhosis associated with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene helps reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress in chronic hepatitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the liver from damage caused by chronic hepatitis by scavenging free radicals and reducing liver inflammation.

Inulin: This prebiotic fiber helps regulate gut bacteria and improve liver function, which can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis by promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria and reducing liver inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate liver inflammation and damage.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to possess hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the liver from damage.
Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce liver inflammation, improve liver function, and promote overall liver health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier.
Weigh 200 grams of the dried material and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian capsules help with chronic hepatitis because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called amarogentin, which has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress. The capsule's bioactive compounds also have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system's response to the virus and reducing damage to liver cells. Additionally, yellow gentian capsules have been found to improve liver function and promote regeneration of damaged tissue, making them a valuable natural adjunct therapy for managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow gentian's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis, thus mitigating damage and promoting healing.

Antiviral: The antiviral properties of yellow gentian capsules can help inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses, reducing their ability to cause further liver damage and slowing disease progression.

Bile duct stimulant: Yellow gentian's bile duct-stimulating property can help improve liver function by increasing bile flow and reducing blockages in the bile ducts, which is particularly important for people with chronic hepatitis who may experience bile duct obstruction.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, particularly gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, have been found to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Gentiana lutea, such as kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate liver damage and promote recovery in chronic hepatitis.

Xanthones: Xanthones, including gentisic acid and gentisic anhydride, have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, contributing to the management of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties.
Rhyzomes: They contain a high concentration of gentiopicroside, an iridoid glycoside with potential hepatoprotective effects.
Leaves: They contain gentiopicroside and other iridoid glycosides, contributing to their hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of sliced roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 20g of gelatin in a small bowl.
Place the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them using a capsule sealer.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and damage. Curcumin has also been found to inhibit the growth of viruses associated with hepatitis, such as HBV and HCV. Additionally, it has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver cells from further damage, promoting overall liver health and function. By reducing inflammation and viral replication, turmeric capsules can help manage symptoms of chronic hepatitis, including fatigue, joint pain, and digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound, curcuminoids, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with chronic hepatitis.

Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Curcumin in turmeric capsules inhibits the activity of COX enzymes, which are involved in the production of pro-inflammatory prostaglandins, thereby reducing liver inflammation and improving symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric's bioactive compounds have been shown to modulate the immune response, helping to reduce the severity of chronic hepatitis by regulating the activity of immune cells and reducing oxidative stress in the liver.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Vasakine (Berbamine): Vasakine, a quaternary alkaloid, has been reported to possess hepatoprotective properties by reducing liver damage and improving liver function in patients with chronic hepatitis.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid saponin, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help in reducing liver inflammation and protecting liver cells from damage associated with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Berberis aristata roots are used to make capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their high berberine content, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: The stems of Berberis aristata are used to make capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their ability to stimulate liver regeneration and improve liver function.
Barks: Berberis aristata barks are used to make capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their high berberine content, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried root of berberis aristata from a reliable source at the end of autumn.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a grinder for 10-15 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered root with 2 parts of a filler like potato starch for uniformity.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine for precision.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain standardized extracts of Phyllanthus amarus, a herb known for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis B virus, reducing inflammation and liver damage associated with chronic hepatitis. Additionally, Phyllanthus has been found to stimulate the production of glutathione, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect the liver from further harm.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Phyllanthus extracts have been shown to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis B virus (HBV), which can help reduce viral load and prevent liver damage.
Immunomodulatory effects: Phyllanthus has been found to modulate the immune response, enhancing the production of cytokines that fight HBV infection and reducing inflammation in the liver.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Phyllanthus capsules help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to chronic hepatitis and its complications.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Phyllanthin: It has been shown to possess anti-viral and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help inhibit the replication of hepatitis B virus and reduce liver inflammation.
Flavonoids: They have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the liver from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids which exhibit hepatoprotective properties.
Stems: They are used as they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress.
Roots: They are used because they are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried whole phyllanthus amarus plants weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a grinding machine.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered plant material with 250 grams of gelatin capsules and 500 grams of starch powder.
Fill the mixture into gelatin capsules that have a capacity of 500 milligrams each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and scarring, which are common symptoms of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, licorice capsules have been found to improve liver function by increasing the production of glutathione, an antioxidant that helps protect the liver from further damage. By reducing inflammation and improving liver function, licorice capsules can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to liver damage and progression of chronic hepatitis.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause liver cell damage and fibrosis (scarring) in chronic hepatitis patients.
Saponins: Soothe and protect the liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress, inflammation, and toxins, which can help reduce liver damage and improve overall liver function.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
Liquiritigenin: A flavonoid compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration.

18β-Glycyrrhetinic Acid: A triterpenoid compound that has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce liver inflammation, suppress the activity of liver-destroying immune cells, and promote liver repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing in chronic hepatitis.
Rhyzomes: They have a similar composition to roots, with glycyrrhizin playing a key role in their medicinal properties.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in the treatment of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for 2 hours.
Sift the extracted powder through a 100-micron mesh to remove impurities and obtain a fine powder.
Mix 1g of the powder with 0.5g of gelatin or vegetarian alternative in a clean stainless steel bowl.
Add 5ml of distilled water to the powder mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the prepared herbal mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because it has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by the disease. The herb's immunomodulatory effects can also enhance immune function, allowing the body to fight off the viral infection that causes chronic hepatitis more effectively. Additionally, ginseng capsules may help improve overall liver health and function, reducing fatigue and improving quality of life for individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis such as fatigue and joint pain.

Immune system modulation: Ginseng has been shown to modulate the immune system by increasing the production of cytokines that help fight off viral infections, which is particularly important for chronic hepatitis patients who may have a weakened immune response.

Antioxidant properties: Ginseng capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can reduce oxidative stress and damage to liver cells, ultimately helping to slow down the progression of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with chronic hepatitis.
Glycosides: Glycosides in Panax ginseng have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective effects by reducing liver enzyme levels and improving liver function in individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby improving liver function and reducing the severity of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are commonly used due to their high concentration of active ginsenosides.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used for their similar ginsenoside content as roots, which helps in treating chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their ginsenoside content, although lower than roots and rhyzomes, they still contribute to the therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Grind 400 mg of dried panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground ginseng powder with 100 mg of rice flour and 50 mg of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Use a gelatin capsule size 00 to fill the mixture into the empty capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
Label the container with the name panax ginseng capsules 400 mg and the date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because it is a powerful blend of herbs that works to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms such as fatigue, nausea, and abdominal pain. The capsules contain a unique combination of bitter compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve nutrient absorption, which can help to repair damaged liver tissue and promote overall liver health. Additionally, the herbal formula has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce scarring and fibrosis in the liver.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the capsules help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting liver health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in the capsules reduce inflammation and swelling in the liver, which can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis such as pain and discomfort.

Immune-modulating properties: The immunomodulatory effects of the capsules help regulate the immune response to viral infections, such as those caused by HBV or HCV, and promote a balanced immune system.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This neolignan compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Diterpenoid lactones (e.g., andrographolide-14-acetate): These compounds have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective effects by inhibiting liver cell apoptosis (programmed cell death) and modulating immune responses to alleviate liver damage.

Ligularin: This sesquiterpene lactone has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities, which help regulate the immune system's response to liver inflammation and promote healing in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high andrographolide content, a key bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for liver health.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the second most commonly used part, as they contain a significant amount of andrographolide and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects.

Roots: The roots are also used, but to a lesser extent, and are valued for their andrographolide content and potential in treating liver diseases, although more research is needed to confirm their efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers or 2-3 teaspoons of dried material.
Clean and dry the harvested material to prevent contamination and spoilage immediately.
Grind 1-2 cups of the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2-3 teaspoons of the powder with 1 teaspoon of gelatin and 1 teaspoon of water to form a uniform mixture for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the mixture and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, two hallmark features of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, curcumin's ability to modulate immune response and scavenge free radicals can help prevent further liver damage and promote healing. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, turmeric capsules may alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, abdominal pain, and yellowing of the skin and eyes commonly associated with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells, reducing oxidative stress and improving overall liver function in patients with chronic hepatitis.

Anti-fibrotic activity: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the formation of fibrous tissue that can lead to liver cirrhosis and scarring, helping to slow down disease progression and improve liver function.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with chronic hepatitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby preventing liver cell damage and promoting liver regeneration.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demethoxycurcumin, another polyphenolic compound, has potent antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can exacerbate chronic hepatitis.

Turmerone: Turmerone, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and promote the healing of liver tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of curcumin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps in reducing liver inflammation and promoting liver health.

Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used to extract curcumin, which has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and reduce oxidative stress in the liver, making it effective in managing chronic hepatitis.

Buds: The buds of Curcuma longa are used for their high content of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting liver regeneration, making them beneficial for chronic hepatitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered curcuma longa with 3 parts of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixed powder and set the capsule size to 0 size for 5 minutes.
Store the curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. These good bacteria help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which are common symptoms of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, chicory capsules have been shown to improve liver function by increasing the production of glutathione, a powerful antioxidant that helps to protect the liver from damage. As a result, chicory capsules may be a useful adjunctive therapy for individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Chicory contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to chronic hepatitis symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and loss of appetite.

Hepatoprotective properties: Chicory's saponins have been shown to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, toxins, and other environmental factors, which may help prevent further liver damage in cases of chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Chicory's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce inflammation and fibrosis (scarring) in the liver, common complications of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, has prebiotic properties that help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can aid in reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function in chronic hepatitis patients.

Caffeic acid phenylethyl ester (CAPE): CAPE, a phenolic compound, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver damage and inflammation, and also modulate the immune response to promote healing and recovery in chronic hepatitis patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain inulin, which is converted to fructooligosaccharides and then fructose, aiding in liver detoxification and regeneration.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting liver health.
Flowers: They are a good source of sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the treatment of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried cichorium intybus flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Crush the dried flowers using a mortar and pestle into a fine powder within 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 95 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with 500mg of the mixture and fill 100 capsules within 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the liver, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate hepatitis symptoms. By regulating liver function and promoting healthy bile flow, chamomile capsules may help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and digestive issues commonly associated with chronic hepatitis. Additionally, its calming effects can also help reduce anxiety and improve overall well-being in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with chronic hepatitis, which can slow down disease progression.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of chamomile capsules neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, a condition often seen in chronic hepatitis patients, which helps protect the liver from further damage.

Cytoprotective property: Chamomile's cytoprotective effects help preserve the integrity of liver cells and tissues, reducing the risk of fibrosis (scarring) and cirrhosis, common complications of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain significant amounts of azulene, a sesquiterpene lactone that has been demonstrated to exhibit hepatoprotective effects by reducing liver damage and improving liver function in cases of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind 30g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 100ml of pure vegetable oil in a clean glass jar and store it at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the herbal extract from the oil and discard the solids.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the herbal extract and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, two key features of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, ginger has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increase the expression of antioxidant enzymes, which helps to protect the liver from further damage and promote its natural healing processes. As a result, herbal ginger capsules may help alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger capsules contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant property: Ginger has antioxidant properties that can help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to liver disease progression and fibrosis (scarring) in chronic hepatitis patients.

Immune-modulatory property: Herbal ginger capsules may modulate the immune system's response to hepatitis infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and enhancing antioxidant defenses.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in the management of liver fibrosis and scarring caused by chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality zingiber officinale rhizomes weighing 250 grams for drying.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm oven at 60°c for 3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 150 grams of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered ginger with 50 grams of silicon dioxide and fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of 750 milligrams of ginger powder and 250 milligrams of filler.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain compounds like alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis such as fatigue, nausea, and jaundice. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been shown to stimulate liver function and improve bile flow, which can help to eliminate toxins from the body and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Asiatic Pennywort capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common characteristic of chronic hepatitis, and alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The capsules contain antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress in the liver cells, thereby protecting against further liver damage and promoting regeneration.
Hepatoprotective: Asiatic Pennywort has been traditionally used to protect and repair liver tissue, which can help prevent or slow down liver fibrosis and cirrhosis associated with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate liver inflammation and damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain high amounts of phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have been found to possess antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, making them effective in combating chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and dry them properly using shade drying or freeze-drying methods within 24 hours.
Grind the dried centella asiatica leaves into fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle to obtain 500 grams of powder.
Mix the powdered centella asiatica with 1000 milliliters of glycerin and 200 milliliters of distilled water in a large bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with one gram each of the herbal blend using an automated capsule filler or manual filling process, yielding 500 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers made of glass or plastic materials at room temperature for up to six months without refrigeration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. This can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, abdominal pain, and jaundice associated with chronic hepatitis, while also promoting liver cell regeneration and protecting against further damage.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Herbal broad-leaved lime capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can aid in alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis (scarring), thereby improving liver function and reducing damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Immune-modulatory property: Herbal broad-leaved lime capsules contain immunomodulatory agents that can help regulate the immune system's response to viral infections, such as those causing chronic hepatitis, which can reduce disease progression and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat liver disorders due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for treating chronic hepatitis.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tilia platyphyllos leaves and stems in early summer for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours to preserve active compounds.
Grind dried tilia platyphyllos into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding 1:1 ratio of leaf to stem.
Mix 500mg of ground tilia platyphyllos with 250mg of carrier herb such as licorice root and fill empty capsules with the blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, allowing for easy dosing twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb has been traditionally used to soothe liver irritations and improve digestion, which is often compromised in individuals with chronic hepatitis. Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves at dawn and clean them thoroughly by rinsing with filtered water.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a warm air circulation oven set at 40°c for 24 hours or until they reach a moisture level of 10%.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding 250 grams.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered melissa officinalis with 1000 milligrams of silicon dioxide and fill #00 capsules, creating 120 capsules in total.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 12 months or refrigerate for up to 24 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other bioactive compounds that have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crataegus monogyna leaves and stems, drying them thoroughly in a warm air circulation dryer for two hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered plant material with one part of vegetable filler such as potato starch or arrowroot powder and one part of gelatin capsules.
Fill each gelatin capsule with 500 milligrams of the prepared herbal mixture, sealing them tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container kept in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Knotweed capsules helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains high levels of saponins, which have been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh polygonum aviculare leaves and stems, drying them in the shade for at least two days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind 30 grams of dried polygonum aviculare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 grams of silica gel and 20 grams of lactose, blending until well combined and free-flowing.
Fill empty hard-shell capsules with the blended mixture, using 500 milligrams per capsule for optimal dosing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, allowing them to be taken as needed or according to the recommended daily dose of two capsules twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves at dawn when dew is still present for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place protected from direct sunlight and moisture.
Grind 20 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground camellia sinensis powder with 10 grams of glycyrrhizae root powder and fill gelatin capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or freeze them for longer preservation.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for chronic hepatitis are natural supplements that aid in alleviating symptoms and potentially slowing disease progression. These capsules contain a blend of herbs that help to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and boost the immune system. The best herbal capsules for chronic hepatitis include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Phyllanthus niruri, Silybum marianum, Andrographis paniculata, Astragalus membranaceus, Schisandra chinensis, Scutellaria baicalensis, Cassia auriculata, and Tribulus terrestris. If left untreated, chronic hepatitis can lead to liver failure, cirrhosis, and even liver cancer, significantly impacting one's quality of life, including fatigue, jaundice, and digestive issues.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic hepatitis that are treated with herbal capsules are liver disorders such as fatty liver disease, cirrhosis, and liver fibrosis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like Milk Thistle, Turmeric, and Dandelion root have been found to improve liver function, reduce inflammation, and promote detoxification. These natural remedies may also help alleviate symptoms associated with chronic liver diseases, including fatigue, joint pain, and digestive issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with chronic hepatitis is a blend of milk thistle, dandelion root, and licorice root. Milk thistle contains silymarin, which helps regenerate liver cells and reduce inflammation. Dandelion root aids in detoxifying the liver, while licorice root soothes the digestive system and reduces oxidative stress. This combination may also include other herbs like turmeric and schisandra, which can help reduce liver damage and promote overall liver health. Regular consumption of these capsules may alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
